action.hypothesis: Because the policy layer validates `param_only` rounds against the current topk family best and only accepts one changed core axis, the deferred Adam callback cannot be isolated cleanly from the `signum@1.6e-3` reference. This round returns to the accepted mainline axis and changes only `LR`, setting `topk/signum/K128/EF12` to `1.7e-3` to resolve whether the useful region is a narrow band just above `1.6e-3` or whether the `1.8e-3` degradation already marks the onset of instability.
action.summary: Stay on the topk mainline and do a pure signum learning-rate refinement: keep `ARCHITECTURE=topk`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `K=128`, and `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` fixed, and move `LR` from `1.6e-3` to `1.7e-3` to answer one question about the local peak of the current stable recipe.
action.self_review: This action is compatible with the stricter single-axis validator because only `lr` changes relative to the current topk reference recipe. It also stays within the active family and avoids another policy rejection while still extracting useful curve-shape information near the current best signum regime.
notes_to_memory: The policy validator for `param_only` currently accepts only one changed core axis relative to the family reference, so optimizer+LR callbacks must be deferred unless the reference optimizer changes.
Round 11 refines the active signum lane locally instead of reopening the rejected Adam callback path.

Judge this run by full curve shape around the late phase: if `1.7e-3` keeps descending smoothly it suggests `1.6e-3` was slightly conservative; if oscillation grows or late FVU degrades, the practical peak is at or below `1.6e-3`.
next_hypotheses: If `signum@1.7e-3` is better and still smooth, one final narrow LR check around `1.75e-3` may be justified before moving on.
If `signum@1.7e-3` is similar but less stable than `1.6e-3`, keep `1.6e-3` as the mainline anchor and stop LR refinement.
If `signum@1.7e-3` is clearly worse, treat `1.6e-3` as the stable optimum and only then reconsider how to legally explore optimizer changes under the validator.

result.description: Stay on the topk mainline and do a pure signum learning-rate refinement: keep `ARCHITECTURE=topk`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `K=128`, and `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` fixed, and move `LR` from `1.6e-3` to `1.7e-3` to answer one question about the local peak of the current stable recipe.
result.self_review: This action is compatible with the stricter single-axis validator because only `lr` changes relative to the current topk reference recipe. It also stays within the active family and avoids another policy rejection while still extracting useful curve-shape information near the current best signum regime.
